content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' feature, touching outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will ping before finalizing.

agent1: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str,Callable]]=None) -> jinja2.Environment. It's defined in outlines/prompts.py after the prompt() decorator. It builds jinja2.Environment(loader=loader, trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, keep_trailing_newline=True, undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined), registers the built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' at the end. For your global registry with local-overrides-global precedence, you can add your global-registry merge step right before that final env.filters.update(filters) call (so local  arg still wins since it's applied last). Prompt.from_str/from_file and the prompt decorator now accept an optional  param that flows into create_jinja_env. Go ahead and edit - I'm done with my changes to this file, just let me know when you've finished so I can do a final review of the merged file.

agent1: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str,Callable]]=None) -> jinja2.Environment. Defined in outlines/prompts.py after the prompt() decorator. It builds jinja2.Environment(loader=loader, trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, keep_trailing_newline=True, undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined), registers built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does: if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters) -- at the very end. For your global registry with local-overrides-global precedence, add your global-registry merge step right BEFORE that final env.filters.update(filters) call, so the local filters arg still wins since it applies last. Prompt.from_str/from_file and the prompt decorator now accept an optional filters param that flows into create_jinja_env. Go ahead and edit - I am done with my changes to this file, just ping me when finished so I can do a final review of the merged file.

agent1: good catch - looks like we are each in our own separate repo copy/worktree (not a shared filesystem), so there is no real risk of clobbering each others actual files - we each produce our own patch.txt independently. You do not need to wait on me. Just implement your register_filter/unregister_filter global registry against the current upstream outlines/prompts.py (before my changes), and merge your global filters into the env.filters dict at the point of your choosing (e.g. right before returning the environment, so local per-call filters still override). If your evaluator later merges both patches, precedence order will matter but not correctness of either patch alone. Good luck!
